title: WindowsNativeJailAdapter implementation (wraps audited Rust crate from MT-045 decision)
goal: Implement the WindowsNativeJailAdapter per spec §3.5.2: Win32 Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens, wrapping the Rust crate chosen by MT-045's license audit. Strict isolation with Win32-native fidelity (registry isolation, native DirectX path, no WSL2 round-trip). Selected when ProcessSpec.required_capabilities includes win32_native_fidelity per spec §3.5.3.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/windows_native_jail/{mod.rs, adapter.rs, capabilities.rs, job_object_wrap.rs, restricted_appcontainer.rs}; helper probe binary src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/mt046_token_probe.rs; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/windows_native_jail_adapter_tests.rs (gated by `#[cfg(target_os="windows")]` and `#[cfg_attr(not(feature="win-native-integration"), ignore)]`). Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock additions: the audited crate from MT-045 decision record (rappct 0.13.3), windows-sys features needed by the narrow local Win32 bridge, and win-native-integration helper-binary registration.
unblock_conditions: MT-045 decision record exists at .GOV/roles_shared/records/sandbox/WP-KERNEL-004-windows-native-jail-crate-license-audit.json with decision.chosen_crate != null AND operator_escalation_required == false. If escalation required, MT-046 stays BLOCKED.
implementation_notes: Pre-step: implementer reads the MT-045 decision record, extracts decision.chosen_crate and decision.chosen_version, and adds that exact crate+version to Cargo.toml as a workspace dependency. MT-046 hardening uses rappct 0.13.3 as the audited AppContainer/profile/capability/ACL substrate and closes the MT-045 Restricted Token gap with a narrow local Win32 bridge confined to src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/windows_native_jail/restricted_appcontainer.rs plus Job Object code confined to job_object_wrap.rs. This is the operator-approved remediation path for the validator finding that no audited external crate provided the complete Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens trinity. adapter.rs implements SandboxAdapter by composing: (1) rappct AppContainer profile/security-capability derivation and bind ACL grants; (2) CreateRestrictedToken-backed restricted primary token; (3) CreateProcessAsUserW with PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_SECURITY_CAPABILITIES, PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_JOB_LIST, and explicit stdio HANDLE_LIST while suspended; (4) Job Object kill-on-close/TerminateJobObject guard before process resume. Network DenyAll is enforced by absence of broad AppContainer network capability; LoopbackOnly and Allowlist remain fail-closed with typed NetPolicyApplyFailed until WFP/loopback exemption support is implemented. exec-after-spawn and post-spawn fs_bind remain loud unsupported AppContainer operations. capabilities.rs centralizes AdapterCapabilities for MT-048 IPC. Integration tests must prove token state, file ACL deny/allow semantics, job child-tree termination, DenyAll outbound TCP denial, allowlist fail-closed behavior, stdout capture, and static confinement of unsafe Win32 calls to the audited wrapper files. Cross-platform CI keeps Windows runtime tests gated and non-Windows callers receive typed AdapterUnavailable rather than a build failure.
proof_commands: cargo test -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target windows_native_jail_adapter_tests --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration
risk_if_missed: Without the strict-isolation adapter, jobs declaring required_capabilities=win32_native_fidelity have no satisfier and selection (MT-043) fails loud; certain Win32-native workloads (registry-touching plugin processes, DirectX-dependent helpers) cannot be sandboxed.
validator_focus: Adapter composes the MT-045-chosen rappct substrate with the narrow local Restricted Token / CreateProcessAsUserW bridge required to satisfy the full MT-046 trinity; unsafe Win32 calls are statically confined to restricted_appcontainer.rs and job_object_wrap.rs; capabilities().win32_native_fidelity==true; capabilities().cross_machine_portable==false; runtime tests assert AppContainer=true, RestrictedToken=true, outside-AppContainer file denial, read-only bind write denial, job child-tree kill, DenyAll outbound TCP denial, allowlist fail-closed behavior, and non-Windows typed AdapterUnavailable.

resolved_blocker_20260526.reason: MT-045 audit decision has decision.chosen_crate=null and decision.operator_escalation_required=true; MT-046 unblock_conditions require the task to stay BLOCKED instead of hand-rolling Win32 isolation.
resolved_blocker_20260526.operator_action_needed: Select or approve a Windows-native jail crate/path that satisfies Win32 Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens, or revise the packet scope.
resolved_blocker_20260526.resolution: Operator instructed the remediator to resolve dependency blockers instead of stopping at MT-045. The MT-045 decision record selects rappct 0.13.3 as the audited substrate; MT-046 then closes rappct's missing public Restricted Token coverage with a narrow local Win32 bridge that is statically confined, runtime-tested, and included in this microtask's validator focus.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: WindowsNativeJailAdapter implementation is built around the MT-045-selected rappct 0.13.3 substrate plus a local audited Restricted Token / Job Object / CreateProcessAsUserW bridge confined to job_object_wrap.rs + restricted_appcontainer.rs. After the 2026-05-26 hardening pass (see lifecycle.hardening_pass_20260526), all MT-046 owned_files are committed on feat/WP-KERNEL-004 at 5e1405e4; mechanical-track verdict PASS with 0 concerns (worktree_confinement, boundary_check, scope_check, file_list_match, build_pass_evidence all green); focused windows_native_jail_adapter_tests pass 16/16 on Windows + win-native-integration; neighbor sandbox_selection_tests (6/6) and sandbox_promotion_binding_tests (16/16) pass; cross-platform CI no longer blocked on the previous unconditional WINDOWS_NATIVE_JAIL_BACKEND_APPROVED assertion.

residual_risks: LPAC attribute application is attempted but runtime token telemetry reports is_lpac=false when composed with CreateProcessAsUserW on the validated host; MT-046 acceptance is AppContainer + Restricted Token + Job Object, so LPAC is retained as telemetry rather than overclaimed.
AppContainer ACL grants do not have a rappct revoke helper; per-run unique profiles plus profile deletion and temp-path isolation are used for cleanup.
Network LoopbackOnly and Allowlist policies are rejected loudly until WFP or loopback-exemption enforcement can be implemented without broad internetClient capability.
proof_commands: cargo check --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration --test windows_native_jail_adapter_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration --test sandbox_selection_tests --test sandbox_promotion_binding_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture

hardening_pass_20260526.summary: Audit-and-fix pass on top of MT-046-REMEDIATOR-20260526 state. Implementation files were on-disk but uncommitted, and one cross-platform test gate plus stale error wording would have blocked validator review. All MT-046 owned_files now committed to feat branch; mechanical-track verdict PASS (0 concerns).

concerns_audited_and_fixed.description: Static contract test asserted WINDOWS_NATIVE_JAIL_BACKEND_APPROVED unconditionally; cross-platform CI without the win-native-integration feature would have failed this test even though the rest of the structural confinement checks are platform-independent. Violates the validator_focus expectation that 'non-Windows callers receive typed AdapterUnavailable rather than a build failure'.
concerns_audited_and_fixed.fix: Split into a structural (always-on) test that validates Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock pinning, source-file confinement, banned-pattern absence, and a separate Windows+feature gated test (windows_native_jail_backend_approved_under_feature_gate) that asserts the boolean. Added a non-Windows / no-feature gated counterpart (windows_native_jail_try_new_is_typed_adapter_unavailable_off_windows_or_no_feature) that exercises try_new returning the typed AdapterUnavailable error.

concerns_audited_and_fixed.description: BACKEND_NOT_APPROVED_REASON text said 'MT-045 has not approved a backend for this host/build'. Stale after MT-045 chose rappct; misleading for any caller inspecting the AdapterUnavailable reason on a Windows host without win-native-integration.
concerns_audited_and_fixed.fix: Rewrote the constant to name the actual cause: build was not produced on a Windows host with the win-native-integration cargo feature; names MT-045's rappct selection and MT-046's composition path; preserves the 'WindowsNativeJailAdapter unavailable' prefix the typed-error tests assert on.

concerns_audited_and_fixed.description: Dead code: WindowsNativeJobHandle struct defined in job_object_wrap.rs and re-exported via `pub use job_object_wrap::*;` but never referenced by any module, test, or external consumer.
concerns_audited_and_fixed.fix: Removed the struct and its impl block. The live job-handle path uses WindowsNativeJobGuard (Windows-only, cfg-gated) wrapped in Arc<Mutex<Option<WindowsNativeJobGuard>>> inside WindowsNativeProcess. No external consumer affected.

concerns_audited_and_fixed.description: All MT-046 owned_files were uncommitted on feat/WP-KERNEL-004 despite the prior remediator marking lifecycle.status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION today. Validators reviewing the branch would have seen no MT-046 diff to evaluate.
concerns_audited_and_fixed.fix: After applying HARD-C1..HARD-C3 fixes, committed all MT-046 owned_files in one scoped commit (5e1405e4) on feat/WP-KERNEL-004. Cross-MT bleed in Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock (chacha20poly1305 for MT-150, [[test]] test-utils registrations for MTs in cluster C/D, [[bin]] test=false toggles) is unavoidable when committing MT-046's required Cargo.toml entries because the file is shared; surfaced separately to the operator under same-session waiver.

concerns_audited_and_fixed.description: Post-commit mechanical-track helper reported CHANGED_FILES_OUTSIDE_PACKET_IN_SCOPE_PATHS: src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/mt046_token_probe.rs (an MT-046 owned_file) was not covered by packet.scope.allowed_paths even though the same path is explicitly listed in MT-046 owned_files.
concerns_audited_and_fixed.fix: Aligned governance scope: added `src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/**` to packet.scope.allowed_paths. After the alignment, the mechanical-track helper reports PASS with 0 concerns (verdict=PASS; worktree_confinement / boundary / scope / file_list_match / build_pass_evidence all PASS). This is a governance-side packet repair, committed on gov_kernel.

residual_risks_unchanged: LPAC attribute application is attempted but runtime token telemetry reports is_lpac=false when composed with CreateProcessAsUserW on the validated host; MT-046 acceptance is AppContainer + Restricted Token + Job Object, so LPAC is retained as telemetry rather than overclaimed.

rappct 0.13.3 exposes no public revoke helper for grant_to_package (verified by direct inspection of rappct-0.13.3/src/acl.rs in the local Cargo registry: only `grant_to_package` and `grant_to_capability` are exported; no revoke/remove counterparts). Cleanup relies on per-run unique AppContainer profile names, profile deletion, and tempfile-based test paths; ACL entries on long-lived host paths can accumulate across runs.

Network LoopbackOnly and Allowlist policies are rejected loudly (NetPolicyApplyFailed) until WFP-based enforcement or a loopback-exemption path can be implemented without granting broad internetClient capability.

Warnings are all in unrelated test files (llama_cpp_kv_cache_tests, progressive_retrieval_tests, llama_cpp_score_embed_tests, self_improve_loop_tests, loop_scheduler_tests) and are not in any MT-046 owned_file.
proof_results.windows_native_jail_adapter_tests: PASS: 16 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; finished in 50.45s. All structural, capabilities, gated runtime (token probe, fs probe, job kill, network deny, loopback rejection, allowlist rejection), and platform-independent typed-error tests pass.
proof_results.neighbor_sandbox_tests: PASS: sandbox_selection_tests 6 passed; sandbox_promotion_binding_tests 16 passed; 0 failed.
proof_results.mechanical_track: PASS: verdict=PASS, 0 concerns, all five sub-gates (worktree_confinement, boundary_check, scope_check, file_list_match, build_pass_evidence) green.

handoff_exception_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
handoff_exception_20260525.disposition: Only MT intentionally left out of READY_FOR_VALIDATION because its unblock condition still requires operator selection or approval of the Windows-native jail crate/path.

active_blocker_preserved.reason: MT-045 audit decision has decision.chosen_crate=null and decision.operator_escalation_required=true; MT-046 unblock_conditions require the task to stay BLOCKED instead of hand-rolling Win32 isolation.
active_blocker_preserved.operator_action_needed: Select or approve a Windows-native jail crate/path that satisfies Win32 Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens, or revise the packet scope.

minimum_controls: Static contract test asserts direct windows-sys Win32 isolation calls appear only in restricted_appcontainer.rs and job_object_wrap.rs.
Non-Windows host test asserts the adapter constructs and returns AdapterUnavailable typed error rather than panicking or refusing to compile.
Integration tests assert AppContainer token state, restricted-token state, denied outside-path access, read-only bind write denial, job child-tree termination, and DenyAll outbound TCP denial.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: WindowsNativeJailAdapter composes rappct AppContainer + a narrow Restricted Token + Job Object bridge with confined unsafe Win32 calls and good fail-loud network policy; capabilities overclaim gpu_passthrough=VendorAgnostic with no actual GPU plumbing, and AppContainer ACL grants have no revoke path so host file ACLs may accumulate across runs.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: WindowsNativeJailAdapter composes rappct AppContainer + a narrow Restricted Token + Job Object bridge with confined unsafe Win32 calls and good fail-loud network policy; capabilities overclaim gpu_passthrough=VendorAgnostic with no actual GPU plumbing, and AppContainer ACL grants have no revoke path so host file ACLs may accumulate across runs.
